skill.name: slo-implementation
skill.description: Define service level indicators, service level objectives, error budgets, burn-rate alerts, and SLO review practices for reliability programs.
skill.summary: Implement measurable SLOs, error budgets, Prometheus rules, and reliability review processes.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution and flagged weak cryptography patterns in plain text. Review found no executable code, no cryptographic implementation, no network access, and no prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The cited backtick locations are Markdown code fences, inline file references, PromQL examples, YAML examples, dashboard mockups, or related skill names. They do not execute commands or instruct the model to run shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are documentation formatting or observability query examples. No shell interpreter, Ruby execution context, script file, or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain SLO prose or Alertmanager annotation text, not cryptographic algorithms. No hashing, encryption, signing, password storage, or cryptographic API usage appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations do not contain crypto primitives or security-sensitive code. The file is instructional Markdown, and these lines are descriptive text or alert messages.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear reliability targets that reflect real user experience. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code define SLIs, SLOs, error budgets, alerts, and review workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains SLI, SLO, and SLA hierarchy for reliability planning.
Provides availability, latency, and durability SLI examples.
Shows error budget calculations and policy thresholds.
Provides Prometheus recording rule and alerting rule patterns.
Outlines Grafana dashboard sections for SLO monitoring.
Defines weekly, monthly, and quarterly SLO review practices.
limitations: Does not include executable automation or deployment scripts.
Uses generic Prometheus metric names that must be adapted.
Does not validate production metrics or alert routing.
Does not provide legal SLA contract language.

use_cases.title: Launch Reliability Targets
use_cases.description: Define availability and latency SLOs before a new service goes live.

use_cases.title: Improve Incident Alerting
use_cases.description: Replace noisy threshold alerts with multi-window burn-rate alerts.

use_cases.title: Review Product Reliability
use_cases.description: Use error budget status to guide release and reliability discussions.

prompt_templates.title: Define My First SLO
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SLO implementation skill to define one availability SLI and one latency SLI for my API service.

prompt_templates.title: Create Error Budget Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SLO implementation skill to create an error budget policy with actions at 100 percent, 50 percent, 10 percent, and 0 percent remaining.

prompt_templates.title: Design Prometheus Alerts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SLO implementation skill to design Prometheus recording rules and burn-rate alerts for a 99.9 percent availability SLO.

prompt_templates.title: Build SLO Review Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SLO implementation skill to create weekly, monthly, and quarterly SLO review workflows for a multi-service platform.

output_examples.input: Create a 99.9 percent availability SLO for an API.
output_examples.output: A clear availability SLI, a 28-day SLO target, expected monthly error budget, and burn-rate alert recommendations.

output_examples.input: Help me reduce alert noise for reliability incidents.
output_examples.output: A multi-window burn-rate alert design that separates fast budget burns from slower reliability degradation.

output_examples.input: Plan a monthly SLO review meeting.
output_examples.output: An agenda covering compliance, budget usage, incident impact, postmortems, and SLO adjustment decisions.

best_practices: Start with user-facing services and user-visible symptoms.
Use multiple SLIs when one metric cannot represent reliability.
Review SLOs regularly and document target changes.
anti_patterns: Do not set 100 percent SLOs for normal production services.
Do not alert on every minor SLI movement without burn-rate context.
Do not copy example metric names without matching local instrumentation.

faq.question: What does this skill help define?
faq.answer: It helps define SLIs, SLOs, error budgets, burn-rate alerts, dashboards, and review practices.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready Prometheus rules?
faq.answer: It provides patterns that must be adapted to your metric names, labels, and alerting standards.

faq.question: Can it help choose an SLO target?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to consider users, business needs, current performance, cost, and competitors.

faq.question: Does it cover latency SLOs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes latency SLI examples based on requests below a defined threshold.

faq.question: Is this a legal SLA drafting tool?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on engineering SLOs and does not provide contract language.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
